summary: Full backend and frontend testing of audio persistence and client-side caching features. 13/18 backend tests pass. All 9 frontend requirements verified via Playwright. The 5 backend failures are all Cache-Control header tests - the backend sets correct headers (confirmed on localhost:8001) but Cloudflare CDN overrides them with 'no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate' on the external URL. Client-side in-memory preloader cache works perfectly.

critical.issue: Cache-Control header is overridden by Cloudflare/CDN proxy. Backend sets 'public, max-age=86400, immutable' (confirmed on localhost:8001) but external URL returns 'no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate'. Browser HTTP caching won't work through CDN.
critical.root_cause: Cloudflare proxy/Kubernetes ingress strips or overrides the Cache-Control response header. The FastAPI backend code in tts_router.py is correct. This is a CDN configuration issue.

action_items: Cache-Control headers are being overridden by Cloudflare CDN on external URL. Consider configuring Cloudflare Page Rules or Cache Rules to allow 'Cache-Control: public' to pass through for /api/tts/* endpoints. The backend code is correct (localhost returns proper headers). Alternatively, document that browser HTTP caching is not available in preview environment - the client-side in-memory blob URL cache (audioPreloader.js) still provides instant playback without HTTP cache.
critical_code_review_comments: audioPreloader.js: React StrictMode causes double-invocation of useEffect, resulting in ~249 preload requests instead of ~126 expected. This is intentional in development but may double load in preview. In production builds, StrictMode double-invoke is disabled.
server.py lifespan: The AI cache count uses a workaround path lookup (__import__('pathlib')) instead of importing database module's _AUDIO_DIR. Consider refactoring to use a clean import.

SettingsContext.jsx: Default settings are never written to localStorage on first visit (only written when user changes a setting). localStorage.getItem returns null on first session. This is fine behavior but worth noting.

success_rate.backend: 72% (13/18 tests pass - 5 Cache-Control header tests fail due to CDN override)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Audio persistence and client-side caching feature fully tested. Key findings: (1) 70 AI cache files and 54 human files confirmed on disk. (2) Preloader fires ~237-256 TTS requests on page load (React StrictMode doubles the expected ~126). (3) H and AI buttons play from in-memory blob URL cache instantly - no new network request. (4) Cache-Control headers work on localhost but are overridden by Cloudflare on the external URL - this is a CDN/infrastructure issue, not a backend code bug. (5) Settings voice toggle persists to 'punjabi_learn_settings' localStorage key. (6) Card flip and audio buttons (no flip trigger) all working correctly.

rca_of_issue.issue: Cache-Control: public headers overridden by Cloudflare CDN
rca_of_issue.root_cause: Cloudflare CDN proxy intercepts the response and replaces the Cache-Control header set by FastAPI ('public, max-age=86400, immutable') with its own policy ('no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate'). This is a Cloudflare configuration issue, not a backend code bug.
rca_of_issue.evidence: curl localhost:8001 returns 'cache-control: public, max-age=86400, immutable'; curl https://punjabi-alphabet.preview.emergentagent.com returns 'cache-control: no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate'
rca_of_issue.impact: Browser HTTP caching (second page load instant playback) won't work. First-load in-memory preloader cache still works perfectly for instant playback within a session.
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Configure Cloudflare Page Rules or Caching Rules to allow Cache-Control pass-through for /api/tts/* endpoints, or use Cloudflare 'Cache Everything' with 'Edge Cache TTL' to cache at CDN level
